prose: Fukui reactivity indices for furan computed via DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier standard, PySCF) in gas phase with charge 0 and multiplicity 1. Fukui indices f⁺ (electrophilicity) and f⁻ (nucleophilicity) were calculated using finite-difference perturbation of the electron count (N±1 states: cation at multiplicity 2, anion at multiplicity 2) and Mulliken population analysis. SCF converged; integrity checks passed. The β-carbons (adjacent to oxygen, indices 3 and 4) are the most electrophilic sites (f⁺ ≈ 1.92–1.93), consistent with furan's known reactivity pattern. Oxygen itself has a negative f⁺ (−1.33), reflecting its electron-donating role in the aromatic system. The dual descriptor confirms the electrophilic character of the β-carbons and the nucleophilic character of oxygen.
